task:
Add implementation plans to Phase 2b (Linux), Phase 3 (Profiles), and Phase 4 (Inheritance) tasks. Run these commands:

bd update SM-coy.2 --description "$(bd show SM-coy.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/backend/linux_keyring.go (//go:build linux)
- Go package: github.com/godbus/dbus/v5 for DBus
- Secret Service API: org.freedesktop.secrets
- Methods: OpenSession (plaintext), CreateCollection, SearchItems, SetAttributes, Clear
- Item attributes: {"xdg.schemas": "envmoat-protected"} for protected, "envmoat-cache" for cache
- Two-item pattern: same as macOS but via Secret Service D-Bus API
- PIN prompt: Secret Service handles prompt via polkit/desktop agent
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.1 (backend interface)
- Acceptance: StoreLUK creates DBus secret items, GetLUK retrieves and decrypts, DeleteLUK removes items" 2>&1

bd update SM-coy.3 --description "$(bd show SM-coy.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/backend/linux_keyring.go (same file, detect KDE)
- Detection: os.Getenv("XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP") contains "KDE"
- KWallet via DBus: org.kde.KWallet interface
- Methods: open(), folderList("envmoat"), writeEntry("envmoat", "luk-protected", value)
- Fallback: if KWallet DBus fails, fall back to Secret Service API
- Dependencies: SM-coy.2 (GNOME Keyring base)
- Acceptance: auto-detects KDE, uses KWallet, falls back to Secret Service" 2>&1

bd update SM-coy.1 --description "$(bd show SM-coy.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/backend/linux_clipboard.go (//go:build linux)
- Detection: os.Getenv("WAYLAND_DISPLAY") != "" -> Wayland -> wl-clipboard
- X11 fallback: exec.Command("xclip", "-selection", "clipboard")
- Wayland: exec.Command("wl-clipboard", "-i")
- Fallback: if neither found, log warning to stderr, return nil error (no-op)
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.1 (backend interface)
- Acceptance: Copy() works on X11 and Wayland, warns if neither available" 2>&1

bd update SM-idf.3 --description "$(bd show SM-idf.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (profiles.go with subcommands)
- Cobra: profilesCmd with list, create, delete, link, unlink subcommands
- list: read index.json profiles section, print name -> bundle mapping + metadata
- create: validate name (regexp ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$), create empty bundle, add to index.json profiles, atomic write
- Bundle name: <slugified-name>.enc
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.7 (encryption), SM-gtv.4 (resolver)
- Acceptance: list shows profiles, create adds to index + creates bundle, name validation works, error on duplicate" 2>&1

bd update SM-idf.1 --description "$(bd show SM-idf.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (profiles.go)
- delete: remove from index.json profiles section, delete bundle file, confirmation prompt
- -y flag: skip confirmation
- Atomic write for index.json
- Dependencies: SM-idf.3 (profiles list+create)
- Acceptance: delete removes profile + bundle, confirmation prompt works, -y skips" 2>&1

bd update SM-idf.2 --description "$(bd show SM-idf.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (profiles.go)
- link: create .envmoat with content "profile: <name>", auto-append to .gitignore, error if marker exists (--force flag)
- unlink: remove .envmoat marker only, don't delete profile/bundle, confirmation prompt
- .gitignore: check if .envmoat already in .gitignore before appending
- Dependencies: SM-idf.3 (profiles list+create)
- Acceptance: link creates marker + gitignore entry, unlink removes marker only, --force overwrites existing marker" 2>&1

bd update SM-1pz.2 --description "$(bd show SM-1pz.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/resolver/ (resolver.go)
- Marker content "disabled" (after trim): return nil bundle, nil error
- load command: on nil bundle, exit 0, no output
- Debug: log "Marker disabled at <path>" to stderr
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.4 (directory walk)
- Acceptance: disabled marker stops walk, load emits nothing, no error" 2>&1

bd update SM-1pz.4 --description "$(bd show SM-1pz.4 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/resolver/ (resolver.go)
- Marker content "profile: <name>" (after trim): parse name, look up in index.json profiles section
- Error if profile not found: "Profile '<name>' not found in index.json"
- Debug: log "Loading profile <name> from marker at <path>"
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.4 (directory walk), SM-idf.3 (profiles)
- Acceptance: profile override loads correct bundle, error on missing profile" 2>&1

bd update SM-1pz.1 --description "$(bd show SM-1pz.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/resolver/ (resolver.go)
- filepath.EvalSymlinks(PWD) for canonical path
- Store canonical paths in index.json
- Test: create symlink to project dir, verify same bundle resolved
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.4 (directory walk)
- Acceptance: symlink and real path resolve to same bundle, no duplicate bundles" 2>&1

bd update SM-1pz.3 --description "$(bd show SM-1pz.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: internal/resolver/ (resolver.go)
- ENVMOAT_WALK_ROOT env var: os.Getenv("ENVMOAT_WALK_ROOT")
- Validation: must be absolute (filepath.IsAbs), must exist (os.Stat)
- Walk stops at walk root instead of /
- Debug: log "Walk boundary: <path>"
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.4 (directory walk)
- Acceptance: walk stops at custom root, error on invalid root path" 2>&1
